title: Don't Get Fooled: AI and Lies

modules.title: The Video That Wasn't Real
modules.sub: A viral clip arrives — but something feels off
modules.description: You step into the role of a junior investigator who receives a shocking viral video that is spreading fast online. You explore why the video feels suspicious and begin building your instincts for spotting when something might not be what it seems.

modules.title: How AI Learns to Fake Things
modules.sub: Discover how AI creates convincing images and voices
modules.description: You go behind the scenes to understand how AI tools like deepfakes and image generators are trained to produce realistic but entirely fabricated content. By seeing how the fakes are made, you start to understand what clues they leave behind.

modules.title: Spot the Clues in Fake Images
modules.sub: Train your eye to catch what AI gets wrong
modules.description: You examine a series of AI-generated images and use a detective checklist to identify telltale signs of manipulation, such as strange hands, blurred edges, or unnatural lighting. Each image sharpens your ability to slow down and look critically before trusting what you see.

modules.title: When the News Gets Twisted
modules.sub: Real stories, fake spin — can you tell the difference?
modules.description: You investigate news headlines and articles that have been distorted, taken out of context, or paired with misleading AI-generated visuals. You practise cross-checking sources and asking key questions to decide whether a story holds up under scrutiny.

modules.title: You're the Fact-Checker Now
modules.sub: Apply your skills to a live investigation challenge
modules.description: You take charge of a simulated fact-checking mission, working through a set of mixed real and fake content using the structured verification process you have built up across the course. Every decision you make must be backed by evidence and explained with clear reasoning.

modules.title: Your Verdict: Real or Fake?
modules.sub: Deliver your final findings and defend your reasoning
modules.description: You present your verdicts on the course's key cases, explaining how you reached each conclusion and what evidence swayed you. This final module challenges you to communicate your thinking clearly, just as a real investigator would when reporting to others.

synopsis: You receive a viral video that could change everything â but is it real? In this story-driven course, you play a junior investigator who must use critical thinking and AI-detection skills to expose fakes before they cause harm.
rationale: Preteens are among the most frequent consumers and sharers of viral content, yet almost no curricula teach them how AI-generated fakes are made, spread, or detected at an age-appropriate level.
learning_outcome: Students can apply a structured verification process to evaluate whether images, videos, or news stories have been artificially generated or manipulated, and explain their reasoning to others.
